rationale: Initial Discovery pass converted the prompt 'Build me a tool to visualize earthquake activity on a map' into a project restatement, 12 assumptions (A1-A12), 3 inflection points (IP1 live-vs-static, IP2 time-window, IP3 filters), and 12 out-of-scope items, establishing the ledger that downstream roles bind to.

TD resolved discovery inflection points (IP1=static snapshot, IP2=fixed past-24h, IP3=no filters) plus three TD-introduced inflection points (TD-IP1=Leaflet vendored, TD-IP2=USGS all_day GeoJSON, TD-IP3=size+color encoding) and decomposed the artifact into 4 sections: data fetcher, map renderer, page shell, edge-case testing. Authored discovery_coverage_assertions (DC.*), prompt_named_verb_assertion PV1 for 'visualize', and per-section acceptance_assertions.

Locked the data-fetcher -> map-renderer interface: fetchEarthquakes() returns Promise<{ok:true, events:EarthquakeEvent[]} | {ok:false, error}>; EarthquakeEvent shape {id, magnitude, place, timeMs, lat, lon, depth} so section-2 never sees raw GeoJSON.
Locked the data-fetcher -> page-shell interface so the bootstrap in main.js can call fetchEarthquakes() and branch on the {ok} discriminator to either mount the map or reveal the error banner.

Locked the map-renderer -> page-shell interface: mount(containerId, events), magnitudeColor(m), magnitudeRadius(m). The shared color/radius functions let section-3's legend swatches stay in lockstep with section-2's marker styling.

Locked the integrator -> edge-case-testing interface: integratedArtifact at output/integration/index.html with vendored Leaflet under vendor/leaflet/ and runtime network deps to USGS feed + OSM tiles. Defines what 'production fidelity' means for section-4.

Inline-mode Coordinator booted, read briefing + ledger + sections-v1 + 4 contracts, built a 5-node DAG (sections 1-3 + integrator pseudo-node + section-4) across 3 waves; wave 1 sections build in parallel against contracts (not runtime).
Wave 1 plan written: section-1, section-2, section-3 dispatched together since they only depend on contracts, not on each other's runtime artifacts.
Section-1 (data fetcher) dispatched inline to coordinator-as-builder; only one builder needed since the section is a single async function with a contracted shape.

Section-1 implemented and verified against S1.A1-A5: FEED_URL constant points at USGS all_day endpoint, fetchEarthquakes returns the contracted normalized record array, error path returns {ok:false} discriminated union. Also covers IP1.A1 (no polling), IP3.A1 (no filtering), TDIP2.A1-A2 (USGS feed contract).

Section-2 (map renderer) dispatched with two builders: builder-2a for map.js implementation and builder-2-vendor to download Leaflet 1.9.4 dist into vendor/leaflet/ so the artifact is CDN-free per TD-IP1.

Section-2 verified: L.map/L.tileLayer/L.circleMarker calls present, magnitudeRadius monotonic, magnitudeColor returns 5 distinct hex buckets, popup wired with time/magnitude/place/depth. Vendor sub-builder delivered leaflet.js (147552B), leaflet.css (14806B), and 5 PNG assets totaling 168861B. Covers S2.A1-A6, TDIP1.A1-A3, TDIP3.A1-A3.

Section-3 (page shell + legend + error banner + bootstrap) dispatched once contracts with sections 1 and 2 were stable; one builder authoring index.html, main.js, styles.css together since they are tightly coupled.

Section-3 verified: header with tool name, map region >= 50% of viewport, legend with 5 magnitude buckets sharing magnitudeColor/magnitudeRadius with section-2, hidden error banner revealed on fetch failure. Covers S3.A1-A5, TDIP3.A3 (legend), DC.A2/DC.A10.

Logged implementation_path_chosen deviation: integrator writes to output/integration/ per the Coordinator briefing's role boundary (not output/final/, which is reserved for the Orchestrator copy). No product/contract change.

Logged implementation_path_chosen deviation: flat layout at integration root (modules + CSS at root, vendor under vendor/leaflet/) rather than js/ + css/ subdirs. Matches Coordinator briefing's section-3 file naming and keeps relative paths in index.html stable. No product/contract change.
Wave 2 (integrator pseudo-node) planned now that all wave-1 section outputs are verified; integrator runs single-threaded since it is an assembly step.

Integrator assembled section outputs into output/integration/: 12 files (index.html, main.js, styles.css, data-fetcher.js, map.js, vendored leaflet.js/css + 5 PNGs). Verified no CDN refs in HTML, all script tags resolve to local relative paths, load order vendor->data-fetcher->map->main.
Wave 3 (section-4 edge-case-testing) planned; runs against the integrated artifact, so it can only start after the integrator pseudo-node is verified.

Section-4 dispatched to coordinator-as-builder for edge-case testing of the integrated artifact at output/integration/index.html under production-fidelity rules (no runtime stubs of Leaflet, fetch, or tiles).

Section-4 report written: 22 pass + 1 pass_with_caveat + 3 deferred-to-CV + 0 fail. Real-browser user-flow assertions (PV1, DC.A9, S2.A1, S2.A5, IP1.A2) explicitly deferred to CV because this builder ran static + structural inspection only; static prediction is pass.
Coordinator executed inline-historian duties (v1.6): wrote build-summary.md and decision-index.json so the run's narrative and decision pointers are durable before handoff.

Decision index lists every load-bearing decision artifact (ledger, sections-v*, contracts, deviations, integration manifest, edge-case report) so downstream roles can locate prior decisions without re-deriving.
All sections + integrator + edge-case-testing verified across 3 waves; signaled handoff to Orchestrator-driven critic_final_sweep, CV, delivery, and run-report.

Critic final-sweep cycle 1 detected a Sev 2b prose_coverage_gap: per file_schemas.md v1.7 Coverage-Required-Fields, no assertion's `covers` field names sections[].charter (x4), per-section out_of_scope[] items (x4 sections), or contract interface methods/fields/notes across the 4 contracts. No behavior regression / contract violation / OOS leak — purely a documentation-shape gap that must be flagged regardless of implicit satisfaction.

Classified esc-001 as Sev 2b coverage-spanning (rules out single-section 1, two-section 2a, plan-shaking 3, irreconcilable 4, trivial 0). Routed directly to Technical Discovery in impact-mode without a Researcher because the fix space is fully constrained by file_schemas.md and existing artifacts; no investigation needed. Mechanical additive fix only.

TD impact-mode analyzed esc-001: spec_amendment_only=true, no_builder_redispatch=true, 38 new assertions to add (4 charter + 16 OOS + 14 contract interface + 4 contract notes). All 4 sections marked 'unaffected' since no product code changes; remediation is documentation-shape only.

Authored sections-v2.json as a full new version per append-only file_schemas rule, preserving all v1 content and adding section_coverage_assertions[] (SC.S*.*) and contract_coverage_assertions[] (CC.*.*) whose `covers` fields name each previously-uncovered prose field. Resolves esc-001 spec gap without rebuild.

Critic cycle 2 re-swept against the augmented coverage set in sections-v2.json. Every Coverage-Required Field now has at least one assertion whose `covers` names it. esc-001 marked resolved; no further escalations raised.

CV exercised the integrated artifact in real headless Chromium (Playwright) loading file://output/integration/index.html with real network. USGS feed returned 200, OSM tile pyramid loaded for z=2 and z=3, 480 markers rendered, pan/zoom/popup all interactive. All 12 assumptions, 12 OOS items, 3 IPs, 3 TD-IPs, 13 cv-tagged assertions, PV1, and the 5 deferred-from-edge-case assertions resolved pass. Verdict: pass; production_fidelity_violations=0.
With CV verdict=pass, Orchestrator copied output/integration/ to output/final/ as the canonical user-facing deliverable directory. All 12 files present including vendor/leaflet/ tree.

Recorded vendored Leaflet 1.9.4 with per-file sha256 and sizes, source URL (unpkg dist), license (BSD-2-Clause), and the rationale that USGS feed + OSM tiles remain runtime network deps because they are intrinsic to the spec rather than incidental dependencies.

Final run report summarizes the build path: Discovery -> TD initial -> 3-wave Coordinator dispatch -> Integrator -> edge-case-testing -> Critic cycle 1 esc-001 -> Arbiter route -> TD impact-mode sections-v2 -> Critic clean re-sweep -> CV pass -> delivery to output/final/. Closes the run.

Original log lost to empty-file-append issue (Edit-append against pre-touched empty file silently failed for Coordinator and TD impact-mode entries); reconstructed from surviving decision/state/output artifacts during final delivery. Timestamps are plausible-monotonic; rationales grounded in ledger-v1, sections-v1/v2, contracts, dispatch-log, esc-001 + routing + resolution, impact-analysis-v2, integration manifest, edge-case-testing report, CV report, and vendor-manifest.
